quail-flambe-argiro.name: Quail Flambé
instructions: Dry quail first on absorbent paper.
Salt and pepper around.
Wrap each with 1 slice of bacon.
Fix with a toothpick.
In a hot pan, add the olive oil.
Sauté the quail around for a few minutes until golden.
Add the garlic broken and let the oil perfume for 1 minute.
We remove it.
Add the grabes cut in half and sauce for 1 minute.
Add the cognac and turn it on to the flame.
Add the thyme and broth.
Lower the heat, cover and simmer for 10 minutes, or transfer to the oven for 15 minutes.
Transfer the quail to a plate and paste the sauce with rhableder into the pan.
Add 1 piece of butter and mix to melt and polish the sauce.
Serve the quails on the plate and pour them with their sauce.
Garnish with grpes, sauté mushrooms and chewed potatoes.
Alternatively, fill them with chopped bacon and their livers and then proceed with the recipe.
A little duck fat in the sauce will give them crispy texture and extra flavor.
It is necessary that quail be well dried on absorbent paper before we proceed to the execution of the recipe.
They do not need much cooking because their meat is tender, without fat and will dry a lot and become hard.
If you want, you can marinate them for a few hours before cooking, in olive oil, herbs, brandy and orange juice.

Traditionally cooked with tomato sauce or lemon salad with oregano. In many areas of Greece during the period of permitted hunting, they are cooked with rice and tomato sauce in the pot. Their meat is tender and sweet.
You can unbleach them, cut them and open them, or fill them with some bacon and their livers.
Quail is a fine taste and is sought after in the circles of hunters and at the festive tables.
Cleanly kept in the freezer for many months inside leave food bags.
Soaked in oil and butter and wrapped with a little bacon enter the oven for only 15 minutes and their taste is indescribable.

quinoa-risotto-with-mushrooms-and-thyme.name: Quinoa Risotto With Mushrooms And Thyme
instructions: Bring 2 cups salted water to boil in medium saucepan. Add quinoa, reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer until tender and water is absorbed, about 13 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat oil in large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and sauté until onion begins to brown, 5 minutes. Add garlic; stir 30 seconds. Add mushrooms and thyme. Sauté until mushrooms are tender, 6 minutes. Add wine; stir until reduced and syrupy, 2 minutes.
Mix quinoa into mushroom mixture; season with salt and pepper. Pass cheese separately.
